spacing.filled_text: Spacing - In primary circuits, 1.5 mm minimum spacing are maintained through air and over surfaces of insulating material between current-carrying parts of opposite polarity and 1.5 mm minimum between such current-carrying parts and dead-metal parts or low voltage isolated circuits. Refer to Illustration __ for areas to verify.

Subject: Re: [External] Re: Intertek Order Qu-01390131-0 processed - Gener8 LLC Project G105581614



Hi Kenneth,



The air tank is a pretty passive component in the system. The function of this component is to provide the consumable (the plastic transparent brick in the drawer) with pressurized air. It is pressurized by the valves and regulators in the instrument, and then releases that air to the front manifold that will direct the air into the proper port. We are finding we might want to reduce the volume of this tank (but keeping the same high pressures). We do not anticipate any changes in function, safety requirements/precautions, material, or placement. We might need to tweak the location of the mounting loop used to secure the tank but no changes to any important component.



Let me know if you have any questions.


Regards,

Roberto

access.filled_text: Accessibility of Live Parts - All uninsulated live parts in primary circuitry are housed within a metal enclosure constructed with no openings other than those specifically described in Sections 4 and 5.

Svc-1 System Shock Shock from touching live part of system with covers removed Catastrophic Likely 3 Service manual warnings, service personnel are trained, and connections are covered. Rare 2 training documentation/ 61010 report
Svc-2 System Pinch hazard while performing maintenance While servicing the internal system, unit activates Severe Possible 2 Service manual warnings, service personnel are trained, interlocks must be over-ridden (personnel are trained to use EMO stop for panel interlock override) and use temporary covers while moving gantry.  Firmware has a position error timeout that will remove power to gantry in the event of a blockage.(under force limit for 61010). Rare 2 training documentation 
Svc-3 System Crush Service operator pulls unit over while installing  Severe Unlikely 2 instructions to tie down system and secure in field service manual Unlikely 2 user manual/

EXPORT NOTIFICATION

grounding.filled_text: Grounding – All exposed dead-metal parts and all dead-metal parts within the enclosure that are exposed are connected to the grounding lead of the power supply cord, which is bonded to the chassis ground.

polarized.filled_text: Polarized Connection - This product is provided with a polarized power supply connection. All single pole switches and fuses are connected only to the ungrounded supply circuit conductor. The block diagram shows a fuse located on the line (L) conductor before entering the power supply, maintaining polarity, and the AC inlet is clearly labeled with L (line), N (neutral), and GND (ground) connections.

internal_wiring.filled_text: Internal Wiring - Internal wiring is routed away from sharp or moving parts. Internal wiring leads terminating in soldered connections are made mechanically secure prior to soldering. Recognized Component separable connectors are acceptable. Wiring passing through metal walls is protected by bushings or grommets. All wiring is minimum ++ AWG, with a minimum rating of 240V, ++°C.

Markings - The product is marked on as follows: The product is marked with the applicant's name, brand name, model number, date of manufacture, electrical ratings, certification marks, and relevant safety symbols and warnings. Markings are applied via adhesive-backed label using matte polyester lamination, with graphics in black on a white background. Markings are required to be legible, free from blurs and scratches, and RoHS compliant. Additional certification marks (such as cETLus and CB) may be present as required by certification bodies.
